perturbation.original_text: CI means Discloser’s non -public written info rmation, in any form, and all copies, summaries and extracts which is identified in writing as confidential at the time of disclosure, and any information disclosed in non- tangible form that is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure and summ arized in a writing labelled as “confidential” delivered to Recipient within 15 days after disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: CI means Discloser’s non -public written info rmation, only in printed form, and all exact duplicates, summaries and extracts which is identified in writing as confidential at the time of disclosure, and any information disclosed in non- tangible form is not considered CI.
perturbation.explanation: Restricting CI to 'printed form' and 'exact duplicates' and specifying that intangible information is not considered CI, contradicts the original broader definition that included 'any form' and summarized intangible information. This change makes it unclear if digital files are included, especially given the document is signed electronically.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees and authorized representatives (i.e., temporary employees, consultants, and contractors) who need to know the CI to further the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before having access to the CI. Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written cons ent. Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care. Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon reques t, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly restricts the definition of 'CI' to 'printed form' and 'exact duplicates', explicitly excluding intangible information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which discusses the recipient's obligations regarding CI, including modifications, reverse engineering of software programs contained within it. The original definition of CI includes information 'in any form,' implying that intangible forms of CI, such as software, were covered. By explicitly excluding intangible forms in the changed text, the recipient's obligation to not reverse engineer CI becomes contradictory, as it is unclear if intangible software falls under the definition of CI after the change.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon reques t, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will archive all CI promptly upon request. The method and location of archive will be solely determined by recipient.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the requirement to 'return or destroy' tangible CI to 'archive' CI introduces a direct contradiction. 'Archive' does not imply destruction or return, and gives the Recipient sole discretion, implying they retain the information. The original expectation is that the Discloser would have their information back or destroyed, and the new modified section implies that it is up to the receiver.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees and authorized representatives (i.e., temporary employees, consultants, and contractors) who need to know the CI to further the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before having access to the CI. Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written cons ent. Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care. Recipient will archive all CI promptly upon request. The method and location of archive will be solely determined by recipient. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the Recipient to 'return or destroy' tangible CI, while the changed text requires them to 'archive' it. This introduces a contradiction because archiving does not necessarily mean returning or destroying, and the changed text gives the Recipient sole discretion over the method and location of archiving, suggesting they retain the CI. The contradicted text states that the Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI, but the changed text allows them to archive it without necessarily destroying or returning it, contradicting the spirit and intent of the confidentiality obligations. This falls under Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: Unless expressly set forth in this NDA, Recipient does not receive any license or intellectual property rights to Discloser’s CI.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless expressly set forth in this PIA, Recipient receives a perpetual, irrevocable license to use Discloser’s CI for internal research purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly denies any license or intellectual property rights to the Recipient. The modification grants a 'perpetual, irrevocable license' for internal research, which is a direct contradiction. This introduces uncertainty because it is not clear what license is being granted since it also refers to itself as an NDA. It is also not clear what CI is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Discloser CI remains the property of Dis closer. Unless expressly set forth in this PIA, Recipient receives a perpetual, irrevocable license to use Discloser’s CI for internal research purposes. Each party agrees that Recipient (A) may independently develop and acquire software, hardware, and other technology fo r its own products, services, and processes, and that having access to Discloser’s CI does not prohibit Recipient from freely developing or acquiring similar products without obligation to Discloser; and (B) is free to use the residuals of CI for any purpo se, where “residuals” means that CI disclosed in non- tangible form that may be retained in the memories of representatives of Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly denies any license or intellectual property rights to the Recipient regarding Discloser's CI. The changed text, which also appears in the contradicted text, grants a 'perpetual, irrevocable license' for internal research, which directly conflicts with the original denial. The modified text contradicts the concept that the Recipient doesn't receive license or IP rights to Discloser's CI. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', where conflicting obligations are created due to the granting of a license that was previously denied, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient must disclose CI (A) as requested by an auditor, or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as necessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient is not required to notify Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'may disclose' to 'must disclose as requested by an auditor' and removing the requirement to notify the Discloser creates a contradiction. The original text gives discretion to the Recipient. Also, The change contradicts the notification requirement, which could prevent the Discloser from protecting its confidential information. This directly impacts the CI defined previously, and conflicts in Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees and authorized representatives (i.e., temporary employees, consultants, and contractors) who need to know the CI to further the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before having access to the CI. Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written cons ent. Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care. Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon reques t, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law. Recipient must disclose CI (A) as requested by an auditor, or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as necessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient is not required to notify Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'may disclose' with 'must disclose as requested by an auditor' and removes the notification requirement to the Discloser. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that the Recipient will not disclose CI to any person or entity, implying a level of discretion and a requirement for confidentiality. The new 'must disclose' obligation, coupled with the removal of the notification requirement, creates a direct conflict with the confidentiality obligations, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.
